summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the least common denominator (LCD)?

The least common denominator (LCD) is the smallest expression that every denominator in the problem divides into evenly. It is found by taking the highest power of each factor that appears in any denominator.

What is a common denominator?

A common denominator is any expression that each original denominator divides into evenly. The LCD is the smallest such expression, but any common multiple of the denominators will work.

What is a complex fraction?

A complex fraction is a fraction where the numerator, the denominator, or both contain fractions themselves. These can be simplified by multiplying by the LCD of the inner fractions.

What is a numerator?

The numerator is the top part of a fraction. When adding or subtracting rational expressions with the same denominator, you combine the numerators while keeping the denominator the same.

What is a denominator?

The denominator is the bottom expression in a fraction. When adding or subtracting rational expressions, the denominators must be the same before you can combine the numerators.

A student walks to school at a rate that covers 1/(x+1) of the distance per minute and jogs back at 1/(x−1) of the distance per minute. What is the total fraction of distance covered in two minutes (one walking, one jogging)?

LCD = (x+1)(x−1). Rewrite: (x−1)/((x+1)(x−1)) + (x+1)/((x+1)(x−1)) = (x−1+x+1)/((x+1)(x−1)) = 2x/((x+1)(x−1)).

A recipe calls for 2/x cups of sugar and 3/(x+2) cups of flour. What is the total amount of dry ingredients?

LCD = x(x+2). Rewrite: 2(x+2)/(x(x+2)) + 3x/(x(x+2)) = (2x+4+3x)/(x(x+2)) = (5x+4)/(x(x+2)).

Two pipes drain a pool. Pipe A removes 1/(x+4) of the water per hour and Pipe B removes 1/(x−2) per hour. What is the combined drainage rate per hour?

LCD = (x+4)(x−2). Rewrite: (x−2)/((x+4)(x−2)) + (x+4)/((x+4)(x−2)) = (x−2+x+4)/((x+4)(x−2)) = (2x+2)/((x+4)(x−2)).

A pharmacist mixes 5/(2x) milligrams from one batch with 3/(4x) milligrams from another. What is the total amount, simplified?

LCD = 4x. Rewrite 5/(2x) as 10/(4x). Then 10/(4x) + 3/(4x) = 13/(4x).

A carpenter has boards measuring 3/(x+1) feet and 5/(x+1) feet. If he joins them end to end and then cuts off a piece measuring 2/(x+1) feet, what is the remaining length?

Add the two boards: 3/(x+1) + 5/(x+1) = 8/(x+1). Then subtract the cut: 8/(x+1) − 2/(x+1) = 6/(x+1).

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Two pipes drain a pool. Pipe A removes 1/(x+4) of the water per hour and Pipe B removes 1/(x−2) per hour. What is the combined drainage rate per hour?

LCD = (x+4)(x−2). Rewrite: (x−2)/((x+4)(x−2)) + (x+4)/((x+4)(x−2)) = (x−2+x+4)/((x+4)(x−2)) = (2x+2)/((x+4)(x−2)).
